mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and tighter capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during tightening cycles, with a mean forward return difference indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter real yields and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 55% over a 4-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that improves corporate profit forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple quarters, with a mean directional accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain stress, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances following a >1.0% INDPRO decline, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below chance levels in walk-forward tests.
